title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: Mention ten (10) financial laws /authorities guiding public sector financial transactions in Nigeria.
model_answer:
I. a. The Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria as amended.
   b. Finance (Control and Management) Act, 1958
   c. Cheque Act, 1957
   d. Audit Ordinance Act, 1956.
   e. Revenue Mobilization and Fiscal Allocation Act, 1982
   f. Public Procurement Act, 2007
   g. Pension Reform Act, 2014
   h. The Annual Appropriation Act
   i. Fiscal Responsibility Act.
   j. Nigeria Revenue Service Act, 2025
   k. The Supplemenatry Appropriation Act.
key_points: a. The Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria as amended.
Revenue Mobilization and Fiscal Allocation Act, 1982
review_notes: Source answer numbering was malformed as "I." and was normalized to question 1.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: Enumerate the six (6) components of IPSAS-accrual general purpose financial statements (GPFS).
model_answer:
2. a. Statement of Financial Position
   b. Statement of Financial Performance
   c. Statement of Cash Flows.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: Highlight six (6) major differences between IPSAS Accrual-based and cash-based accounting.
model_answer:
3. (a) Timing of Recognition:
Under Accrual IPSAS, revenues and expenses are recorded when they are earned or incurred, irrespective of when cash changes hands.
Under Cash IPSAS, transactions are only recorded when cash is actually received or paid.
   (b) Tracking of Receivables and Payables:
Accrual IPSAS accounts for outstanding receivables and payables.
Cash IPSAS does not track payables or receivables, meaning outstanding debts aren't visible on the primary statements.
   (c) Asset Depreciation:
Accrual IPSAS mandates capitalizing fixed assets and spreading their cost over their useful lives via depreciation.
Cash IPSAS typically expenses the full cost of an asset at the time of purchase and does not record depreciation.
   (d) Financial Statements Provided:
Accrual IPSAS requires a full suite of reports, including a Statement of Financial Position (Balance Sheet), Statement of Financial Performance (Income Statement), and Statement of Cash Flows.
Cash IPSAS primarily requires a Statement of Cash Receipts and Payments.
   (e) Accuracy and Performance Representation:
Accrual IPSAS follows the matching principle, providing a much clearer and more accurate picture of a government's or agency's financial health, performance, and true cost of operations.
Cash IPSAS focuses strictly on liquidity and cash availability rather than economic performance.
    (f) Complexity and Implementation:
Accrual IPSAS is more complex, requiring advanced IT systems, professional training, and double-entry bookkeeping.
Cash IPSAS is simpler, follows a single-entry cash system, and is easier to implement for governments with limited resources.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: Mention the objectives of financial statements.
model_answer:
4.
a. Accountability: Ensuring that public funds are utilized in accordance with legislative mandates, appropriation acts, and statutory budgets.
b. Evaluating Performance: Helping citizens and policymakers assess the economical and efficient delivery of public services.
c. Resource Allocation & Sustainability: Detailing how activities are financed and assessing the entity's capacity to continue providing services and paying its liabilities.
d. Transparency: Providing a clear, comprehensive, and comparable view of the government's entire financial operations.
key_points: Accountability: Ensuring that public funds are utilized in accordance with legislative mandates, appropriation acts, and statutory budgets.
Evaluating Performance: Helping citizens and policymakers assess the economical and efficient delivery of public services.
Resource Allocation & Sustainability: Detailing how activities are financed and assessing the entity's capacity to continue providing services and paying its liabilities.
Transparency: Providing a clear, comprehensive, and comparable view of the government's entire financial operations.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: What are the pieces of financial information that Users of Government financial report need?
model_answer:
5. GOvernment financial information is needed to:
a. know the sources and types of revenues of Government.
b. evaluate the allocation and use of resources.
c. ascertain the extent to which available are adequate enough to sustain cost of operations.
d. evaluate the financial performance of the government's Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs) and Government Business Enterprises. (GBEs).
e. know the economic impact of the Government's policies and programmes on the citizens.
f. Ascertain public funds are used in line with extant rules and regulations.
g. evaluate whether the Government is able to meet its short-term and long-term financial obligations.
key_points: know the sources and types of revenues of Government.
evaluate the allocation and use of resources.
ascertain the extent to which available are adequate enough to sustain cost of operations.
evaluate the financial performance of the government's Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs) and Government Business Enterprises. (GBEs).
know the economic impact of the Government's policies and programmes on the citizens.
Ascertain public funds are used in line with extant rules and regulations.
evaluate whether the Government is able to meet its short-term and long-term financial obligations.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: Briefly describe the benefits of Accountability and Transparency.
model_answer:
6.a. BENEFITS OF ACCOUNTABILITY
Accountability means taking onwership and being answerable for one's action and its consequences wthether positive or negative.
Benefits of Accountability
a. It promotes trust.
b. Improves performance and prodcutivity
c. It enables errors to be figured out and identification of areas for improvements.
d. It promotes fairness and minimises bias.
e. It enhances dispute resolutions.
6.b. BENEFITS OF TRANSPARENCY
Transparency means openness and honesty to all stakeholders.
a. It fosters credibility.
b. It promotes ethical conduct
c. It enhances informed decision.
d. It boosts employees' engagement.
e. It promotes public accountability.
key_points: It enables errors to be figured out and identification of areas for improvements.
It promotes fairness and minimises bias.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: State some objectives of the Federal GOvernment's Financial Transparency Policy.
model_answer:
7. Objectives of the Federal GOvernment's Financial Transparency Policy.
a. To enable the public to report any observed financial wrongdoing in a public sector entity.
b. To promote a fight against corruption.
c. To facilitate the freedom of information implementation.
d. To promote the government's whistle Blower Policy.
e. To make financial information available to the general public on a timely basis.
key_points: To enable the public to report any observed financial wrongdoing in a public sector entity.
To promote a fight against corruption.
To facilitate the freedom of information implementation.
To promote the government's whistle Blower Policy.
To make financial information available to the general public on a timely basis.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: What are the roles of OAGF in the Federal Government's Financial Transparency Initiative?
model_answer:
8. Roles of OAGF in the Federal GOvernment's Financial Transparency Initiative (FTI)
a. To mainitain the transparency web portal, i.e. wwww.opentreasury.gov.ng.
b. To publish all financial information on the Government-affliated payment platforms, i.e. GIFMIS and REMITA.
c. To maintain FTI Help-Desk for attending to queries on an MDA's financial allegations.
d. For financial transparency monitoring and compliance among MDAs.
e. To recommend sanctions for any MDA's non-compliance with FTI.
key_points: To mainitain the transparency web portal, i.e. wwww.opentreasury.gov.ng.
To publish all financial information on the Government-affliated payment platforms, i.e. GIFMIS and REMITA.
To maintain FTI Help-Desk for attending to queries on an MDA's financial allegations.
For financial transparency monitoring and compliance among MDAs.
To recommend sanctions for any MDA's non-compliance with FTI.

title: Permanent Secretaries Oral Prep
prompt: What are the roles of MDAs in the Federal Government's Financial Transparency Initiative?
model_answer:
9. Roles of MDAs in the Federal GOvernment's Financial Transparency Initiative (FTI)
a. To ensure that Payments that are processed through GIFMIS and REMITA are properly narrated.
b. To designate an officer not below the Head of Finance and Accounts to be responding to questions on FTI.
c. To ensure that all revenues are remitted through the GIFMIS Revenue Reference Number (GRRN).
d.To ensure that transfers to MDAs' Sub-TSA domiced with CBN have proper narrations of amounts and beneficiaries.
e. To be rsponsible for any wrong description of financial transactions.
key_points: To ensure that Payments that are processed through GIFMIS and REMITA are properly narrated.
To designate an officer not below the Head of Finance and Accounts to be responding to questions on FTI.
To ensure that all revenues are remitted through the GIFMIS Revenue Reference Number (GRRN).
To ensure that transfers to MDAs' Sub-TSA domiced with CBN have proper narrations of amounts and beneficiaries.
To be rsponsible for any wrong description of financial transactions.
